question.text: Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on the sale of futures contracts is:

answer.text: 0.0125% of the turnover (contract value), payable by the seller
0.1% of the turnover, payable by both buyer and seller
0.0625% of the premium, payable by the buyer
No STT is applicable on futures contracts

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** STT on futures: - Rate: 0.0125% (1.25 paise per Rs. 100) - Payable on: the selling side of the futures contract (turnover/contract value) - Payable by: the seller
For example, if the contract value is Rs. 10,00,000, STT = 10,00,000 x 0.0125% = Rs. 125.
**The Trap:** The STT rate for futures (0.0125%) is different from options selling (0.0625% on premium) and options exercise (0.125% on settlement price). Do not confuse these rates.
**Cross-Exam Context:** STT rates for different derivative transactions are directly tested factual questions.
**Key Takeaway:** Futures STT: 0.0125% on turnover (sell side). Options sell: 0.0625% on premium. Options exercise: 0.125% on settlement.
